title: Food Web Plots with webplotviz
text: webplotviz() creates static food web diagram Rpath object. Nodes represent functional groups fleets; node size reflects biomass edges represent predator–prey interactions fishing pressure. y-axis fixed trophic level.

title: Food Web Plots with webplotviz
text: AB.params small fictional ecosystem included Rpath package. good starting point renders quickly.
code: # Build the Rpath balanced model Rpath.obj <- rpath(AB.params, eco.name = "Anchovy Bay")  # Plot with default settings webplotviz(Rpath.obj)

title: Food Web Plots with webplotviz
text: Use h_spacing spread nodes horizontally node_size_min / node_size_max control range node sizes.
code: webplotviz(   Rpath.obj,   h_spacing     = 5,   node_size_min = 2,   node_size_max = 20,   text_size     = 4 )

title: Food Web Plots with webplotviz
text: Two built-palettes available: "rpath_pal_dark" (default) "rpath_pal_light". can also supply custom vector hex colors.
code: webplotviz(Rpath.obj, groups_palette = "rpath_pal_light") webplotviz(   Rpath.obj,   groups_palette = c("#264653", "#2a9d8f", "#e9c46a", "#f4a261", "#e76f51"),   fleet_color    = "#023e8a" )

title: Food Web Plots with webplotviz
text: Two parameters control visual style rendering speed plot. labels (default TRUE) draws group name labels using ggrepel avoid overlap. Label placement largest single rendering cost — 54-group model accounts roughly half total render time. Set labels = FALSE want fast preview clean export ’ll annotate separately. gradient (default FALSE) draws edge prey--predator colour gradient instead fixed line.col colour. adds visual cue interaction direction modest render cost.
code: # labels = FALSE: fastest render, useful for large-web previews webplotviz(Rpath.obj, labels = FALSE) # gradient = TRUE: prey–predator colour gradient on edges webplotviz(Rpath.obj, gradient = FALSE)

title: Food Web Plots with webplotviz
text: Nodes coloured community membership, detected igraph’s community detection algorithms. cluster_method parameter accepts four options:
code: webplotviz(Rpath.obj, cluster_method = "fast_greedy", labels = FALSE) +   ggplot2::ggtitle("fast_greedy (default)") webplotviz(Rpath.obj, cluster_method = "louvain", labels = FALSE) +   ggplot2::ggtitle("louvain") webplotviz(Rpath.obj, cluster_method = "walktrap", labels = FALSE) +   ggplot2::ggtitle("walktrap")

what: Large food web — Eastern Bering Sea
title: Food Web Plots with webplotviz
text: food webs 20 functional groups, plotting takes longer. recommended workflow : Use labels = FALSE avoid ggrepel label-placement cost. Assign plot object save ggplot2::ggsave() rather rendering inline.  save high-resolution version:
code: # Build the Eastern Bering Sea model EBS.obj <- rpath(Ecosense.EBS, eco.name = "Eastern Bering Sea") EBS.plot <- webplotviz(   EBS.obj,   h_spacing     = 3,   text_size     = 2.5,   node_size_min = 2,   node_size_max = 30,   labels        = FALSE ) #> Your food web object has more than 20 functional groups. #>      #> Plotting to the RStudio window will take a while, please be patient... #>     Refer to the examples for large food webs. EBS.plot ggplot2::ggsave(   "EBS_foodweb.png",   EBS.plot,   width  = 16,   height = 10,   dpi    = 300 )

title: Simulation Plots with rsimplotviz
text: rsimplotviz() creates interactive plotly time series Rsim output object. can plot species focus individual groups, toggle absolute relative biomass.

title: Simulation Plots with rsimplotviz
text: use fictional R Ecosystem (REco.params) bundled Rpath.
code: # Build the balanced Rpath model REco <- rpath(REco.params, eco.name = "R Ecosystem")  # Create an Rsim scenario and run it for 50 years scene       <- rsim.scenario(REco, REco.params, years = 1:50) Rsim.output <- rsim.run.patched(scene, years = 1:50)

title: Simulation Plots with rsimplotviz
text: spname = "" (default), species plotted relative biomass (biomass relative starting value). Use legend toggle individual species .

title: Simulation Plots with rsimplotviz
text: Switch "rsim_pal_light" softer, pastel look.

title: Simulation Plots with rsimplotviz
text: examine one species original biomass units, pass name spname set rel_bio = FALSE.
code: rsimplotviz(Rsim.output, eco.name = "R Ecosystem",             spname  = "Foragefish2",             rel_bio = FALSE)

title: Simulation Plots with rsimplotviz
text: Setting rel_bio = TRUE rescales single species starting biomass, making easy compare trends across species scenarios.
code: rsimplotviz(Rsim.output, eco.name = "R Ecosystem",             spname  = "Foragefish2",             rel_bio = TRUE)

title: Simulation Plots with rsimplotviz
text: Supply vector hex colors. rsimplotviz() interpolate provide fewer colors species.
code: my_pal <- c("#264653", "#2a9d8f", "#e9c46a", "#f4a261", "#e76f51",             "#023e8a", "#8ecae6", "#219ebc", "#023047", "#ffb703")  rsimplotviz(Rsim.output, eco.name = "R Ecosystem", palette = my_pal)

title: Beautiful plots for Ecopath/Rpath package
text: Vizualizations Rpath package.

title: Beautiful plots for Ecopath/Rpath package
text: install package build vignettes locally experience issues installing package using remotes don’t need vignettes locally please use alternative Figure 1. Gulf Alaska marine food web.
code: remotes::install_github("biadias/rpathviz", build_vignettes=TRUE) pak::pak("biadias/rpathviz") # Build the GOA GOA.obj <- rpath(Ecosense.GOA, eco.name = "Gulf of Alaska") GOA.plot <- webplotviz(   GOA.obj,   h_spacing     = 3,   text_size     = 2.5,   node_size_min = 2,   node_size_max = 30 )

title: Beautiful plots for Ecopath/Rpath package
text: Lucey, S. M., Gaichas, S. K., & Aydin, K. Y. (2020). Conducting reproducible ecosystem modeling using open source mass balance model Rpath. Ecological Modelling 427(2020), 109057. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ecolmodel.2020.109057.

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — rsimplotviz
text: Interactive plots Rsim plot using plotly:: package tidyverse:: add another paragraph

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — rsimplotviz
code: rsimplotviz(   Rsim.output,   eco.name = NULL,   spname = "all",   rel_bio = FALSE,   palette = "rsim_pal_dark" )

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — rsimplotviz
text: Rsim.output object class Rsim. eco.name Optional name ecosystem. spname character vector species names plot. "", species plotted rel_bio use default FALSE. rel_bio Logical value whether plot single group biomass relative absolute value. palette character vector colors use.

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — rsimplotviz
text: interactive plotly object

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — rsimplotviz
text: rsim.plot function Rpath Kerim Aydin

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — rsimplotviz
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # create the Rsim object runObj <- Rpath::rsim.scenario(Rpath::rpath(Rpath::REco.params), Rpath::REco.params, years= 1:50) Rsim.output <- Rpath::rsim.run(runObj, years= 1:50)  # plot the Rsim object two default color blind friendly palettes rsimplotviz(Rsim.output, eco.name= "Anchovy Bay", spname = "all", palette= "rsim_pal_dark") rsimplotviz(Rsim.output, eco.name= "Anchovy Bay", spname = "all", palette= "rsim_pal_light") rsimplotviz(Rsim.output, eco.name= "Anchovy Bay", spname = "Foragefish2", rel_bio=FALSE, palette= "rsim_pal_light")  } # }

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — webplotviz
text: Interactive plots Rsim plot using plotly:: package tidyverse:: add another paragraph

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — webplotviz
code: webplotviz(   Rpath.obj,   eco.name = attr(Rpath.obj, "eco.name"),   line.col = "grey",   h_spacing = 3,   node_size_min = 1,   node_size_max = 30,   fleet_color = "#B40F20",   groups_palette = "rpath_pal_dark",   text_size = 3,   max.overlaps = 50,   gradient = TRUE,   labels = TRUE,   cluster_method = c("fast_greedy", "louvain", "walktrap", "edge_betweenness") )

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — webplotviz
text: Rpath.obj object class Rpath rpath(). eco.name Optional name ecosystem.  Default eco.name attribute rpath object created running rpath(). line.col description. h_spacing horizontal spacing multiplier, spreads nodes horizontally. node_size_min scale_value() Minimum value new scale (default 1). node_size_max scale_value() Maximum value new scale (default 30). fleet_color single color fleet nodes. groups_palette color palette non-fleet groups. Two palette options rpath_pal_dark rpath_pal_light. text_size size text labels. max.overlaps maximum number overlapping labels allowed ggrepel. Increase denser label coverage, decrease reduce clutter. Default 50. gradient Logical. TRUE(default), edges drawn colour gradient (prey--predator) using after_stat(index). FALSE, edges drawn fixed line.col colour, renders significantly faster large food webs. labels Logical. TRUE (default), group names drawn using ggrepel avoid overlap. Set FALSE skip labels entirely, roughly halves render time large food webs. cluster_method Community detection algorithm used colour node clusters. One "fast_greedy" (default), "louvain", "walktrap", "edge_betweenness". "fast_greedy" "louvain" recommended large food webs; "edge_betweenness" accurate significantly slower.

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — webplotviz
text: Returns plot visualization food web.

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — webplotviz
text: webplot original function Rpath Kerim Aydin

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — webplotviz
text: Caution: large food webs see examples.

title: Interactive plots for Rsim — webplotviz
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Read in Rpath parameter file, generate and name model object Rpath.obj <- Rpath::rpath(Rpath::AB.params, eco.name = "Anchovy Bay") # Plot food web diagram with all groups labeled, including fleets webplotviz(Rpath.obj, h_spacing = 3, text_size = 3)  # For large food webs, use labels = FALSE to speed up rendering, then save # with ggsave() for best results. # 1) assign the plot to an object # 2) use ggplot2::ggsave() to save the plot for a fast visualization Rpath.obj <- Rpath::rpath(Rpath::Ecosense.EBS, eco.name = "Eastern Bering Sea") wp <- webplotviz(Rpath.obj, h_spacing = 3, text_size = 3,   node_size_min = 1, node_size_max = 50, labels = FALSE) ggplot2::ggsave("figures/EBSfoodwebplot2.png", wp, width = 16, height = 10)   } # }
